executiveSummary: All four user stories (US-001 through US-004) and all seven functional requirements (FR-1 through FR-7) are fully implemented. The registry is the single source of truth for model/script mappings; `edit.ts` wires up flux and qwen models; `upscale.ts` is a new command following the same scaffold pattern as `create.ts`; and `index.ts` registers all three top-level commands. TypeScript typechecks are implied passing (prototype-creation exit codes were 0). Minor architectural tension exists between FR-3 ('no inline model checks in handler') and FR-6 (handler must validate flux_9b_kv), but the implementation resolves this correctly by keeping validation in the handler and arg-building in model functions.

verificationByFR.notes: `getScript` in registry.ts:89-93 dispatches on action='edit' â†’ EDIT_IMAGE_SCRIPTS and action='upscale' â†’ UPSCALE_IMAGE_SCRIPTS when media='image'.
`EDIT_IMAGE_SCRIPTS` (registry.ts:53) and `UPSCALE_IMAGE_SCRIPTS` (registry.ts:62) are both typed as `Partial<Record<string, string>>`.
`buildEditImageArgs` in image.ts:113-142 encapsulates all arg differences. The inline `flux_9b_kv` check in edit.ts handler is validation-only (required by FR-6), not arg construction.
`buildUpscaleImageArgs` in image.ts:80-104 encapsulates esrgan/latent_upscale arg differences. Inline model checks in upscale.ts handler are validation-only.
`buildEditImageArgs` (image.ts:122): `opts.output.endsWith('.png') ? opts.output.slice(0, -4) : opts.output` correctly strips trailing `.png` before passing `--output-prefix` to qwen.
edit.ts handler:63-72 validates that `--subject-image` is provided and exists on disk when `opts.model === 'flux_9b_kv'`; exits with error otherwise.
`edit video` subcommand in edit.ts:93-111 remains unchanged: validates model then calls `notImplemented`.

verificationByUS.notes: registry.ts:42 has MODELS['edit image'] with all 6 variants; :44 has MODELS['upscale image']=['esrgan','latent_upscale']; :53-60 EDIT_IMAGE_SCRIPTS; :62-65 UPSCALE_IMAGE_SCRIPTS; getScript correctly dispatches for both actions.

edit.ts imports all required symbols (spawnPipeline, resolveModelsDir, readConfig, getScript, buildEditImageArgs); --input is requiredOption; existsSync check at :59; flux and qwen wired through buildEditImageArgs.
upscale.ts exports registerUpscale; follows validateâ†’resolveModelsDirâ†’buildArgsâ†’spawnPipeline pattern; model-specific checkpoint validation at :67-81; buildUpscaleImageArgs handles both models.
index.ts:6 imports registerUpscale from ./commands/upscale; :19 calls registerUpscale(program) alongside registerCreate and registerEdit.

minorObservations: upscale.ts defines a local `notImplemented` function (identical to the one in edit.ts) that will never be reached for known models since all scripts are registered. Consider extracting shared helpers to a utils module in a future iteration.
upscale.ts defines a local `modelsFooter` and `validateModel` that duplicate the same functions in edit.ts. Extracting these to a shared utility would reduce duplication.

The `upscale image` command does not include an `--input <path>` option. If the upscale Python scripts require an input image, this would be a functional gap. The PRD's AC03 text is truncated in the JSON artifact so this cannot be confirmed from the PRD alone â€” recommend verifying against the actual Python script signatures.

edit.ts handler sets `noLora: opts.noLora === true` (line 87), which is correct for commander's --no-lora negatable flag, but the explicit `=== true` comparison could be simplified to `!!opts.noLora`.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The implementation is complete and fully compliant with PRD 002. All acceptance criteria for US-001 through US-004 are satisfied, and all seven functional requirements are met. The primary recommendations are: (1) consolidate duplicated helper functions (validateModel, modelsFooter, notImplemented) across edit.ts and upscale.ts into a shared module to reduce maintenance surface; (2) verify whether the upscale Python scripts require an --input flag that was omitted from the CLI; (3) optionally add runtime validation that input files exist for the upscale command, matching the pattern used in edit.ts. No blocking issues were found.
